postData.contentHtml:
Senator Bato Dela Rosa, a key figure from the previous administration's 'drug war,' is confirmed by the Bureau of Immigration (BI) to have no recent records of traveling outside the Philippines. This statement comes amidst an Immigration Lookout Bulletin Order (ILBO) issued against him, an order that, while not prohibiting departure, signals law enforcement agencies of his movements should he attempt to leave the country.

Unconfirmed reports had suggested Senator Dela Rosa had fled to New York City, allegedly aided by a group supportive of Vice President Sara Duterte. However, these claims have been refuted by official immigration records.

The senator faces an International Criminal Court (ICC) warrant for his arrest due to alleged crimes against humanity of murder during his tenure as Philippine National Police (PNP) chief. The Supreme Court did not issue a temporary restraining order against the ICC warrant, prompting Justice Secretary Frederick Vida to issue an order for his arrest.

The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has declared Senator Dela Rosa 'armed and dangerous,' an assertion his lawyer, Israelito Torreon, has strongly denied. Reports indicate that Dela Rosa slipped out of the Senate building on May 14 and has since remained in hiding.

In a direct appeal, NBI Director Melvin Matibag has urged the senator to surrender. Tracker teams from the Philippine National Police (PNP) have joined a nationwide manhunt to locate and apprehend him. Furthermore, the Department of Justice, through Spokesperson Polo Martinez, has revealed that the government has applied for an Interpol Red Notice against Senator Dela Rosa, intensifying the efforts to bring him into custody. Once arrested, he is expected to be turned over to the Department of Justice (DOJ).

Public sentiment on social media regarding the 'Bato Dela Rosa Immigration' topic shows a largely positive reception, with some ongoing debate surrounding the situation.
postData.title: Senator Bato Dela Rosa Remains in the Philippines Amidst ICC Warrant and Manhunt
postData.summary: Despite swirling rumors of his escape, Senator Bato Dela Rosa has no recorded travel outside the Philippines, as confirmed by the Bureau of Immigration. He is currently a subject of a nationwide manhunt following an International Criminal Court arrest warrant related to the past administration's 'drug war.'

sources.title: No record showing Bato dela Rosa left Philippines — Immigration

NBI chief to Bato dela Rosa: 'It's better for you to just surrender'
Bato dela Rosa still in PH as police deploy tracker teams
